column1: # Official ASN.1 BER/DER length-octet test vectors.
# Source: ITU-T Rec. X.690 (ISO/IEC 8825-1), Section 8.1.3 "Length octets".
# - Short form (8.1.3.4): one octet, bit 8 = 0, length in bits 7..1; for lengths 0..127.
# - Long form (8.1.3.5): first octet bit 8 = 1, bits 7..1 = number of subsequent length octets,
#   followed by the length, base 256, most significant octet first. The 435 -> 82 01 b3 row is the
# These are the definite, minimal-length encodings; they are valid for BER, CER and DER alike.
# The BER-only indefinite form (single octet 0x80) is NOT a determinate length value and is omitted.
